basis: Code-grounded canonical profile; two live selection loops, exact Eshkol-AD biologic learning, base-population flora-gradient chemotaxis, a live VQE drive-conflict resolver (GATE-VQE-RESOLVE), and a predictive-metacognition faculty that fits an EXACT arbitrary-order Taylor jet and gates decisiveness by its validated remainder (GATE-PREDICTIVE-METACOG) — six accumulated gate-backed mechanisms now substantiate the self-scored cognition ceiling; classical statevector, not a QPU; no peer-reviewed external validation yet
Canonical CA; rich pattern ecology; no agent cognition
Emergent flocking from local rules
Human-selected genotype-to-form evolution
VM-resident programs competing; later evolutionary variants
Self-replicating mutable programs competing for CPU/memory
Open-source digital organisms and evolutionary dynamics
Evolved agents with neural nets, vision, metabolism, mating, predation
Bodies and brains evolve in 3D simulated creatures
Genetic algorithms evolve 3D morphology and neural controllers
Genetics, biochemistry, neural brains, learning, language-like interaction
Evolving swimmers in a genetic pond
Morphology and mate preference drive swimbot evolution
Bottom-up artificial society with agents, metabolism, resources
Holland adaptive agents and resource exchange
Reusable toolkit for large multi-agent experiments
3D multi-agent and artificial-life simulation environment
Collaborative interactive evolution of images/CPPNs
Modular framework for digital evolution experiments
Continuous CA with diverse autonomous lifeforms
Learned local update rules for growth and regeneration
Open-source virtual C. elegans organism effort
Open-ended generation of challenges and solutions
Benchmark for co-optimizing soft robot body and control
CUDA ecosystem for digital organisms and prebiotic evolution
Automated search for artificial-life simulations using foundation models

Brian Silverman's 4-state Moore-neighborhood CA that models electron flow in wires; Turing-complete (a full RISC computer was built in it) but has no heredity, agents, evolution, or physics. Verified: real, distinct computational CA; scores hold under rubric (no A-life properties, consc0, substrate1 correct).

Chris Langton's A-Life turmite: one agent on a grid, two rules, that self-organizes chaos into an emergent periodic 'highway'; single agent, no reproduction, mutation, or learning. Verified real/distinct; scores defensible (single-agent, no heredity/ecology/cognition).

Langton's 8-state 86-cell loop that copies its genetic instruction stream out an arm to spawn a daughter loop; landmark 'third-generation' self-replicator but blind copying with no mutation/selection or open-ended novelty. Verified real/distinct; repro4 defensible (real self-replication of an instruction stream, below Avida's mutation+selection 5), peer5 justified by foundational status.

The 29-state CA that first proved machine self-replication is logically possible via a tape-driven universal constructor+copier; foundational to all A-Life reproduction theory but abstract, no ecology/physics/rendering. Verified real/distinct; scores hold (repro4 for tape-driven self-reproduction, open2 slightly generous but within tolerance given universal-construction capacity, instr1/vis2 correct for a mostly-theoretical CA rarely fully simulated).

Wolfram's Class-4 elementary 1D CA with interacting localized gliders; proven Turing-complete by Cook (2004) via cyclic tag systems, but a pure computational CA with no agents, heredity, or embodiment. Verified real/distinct; all scores defensible (computational CA, no A-life axes, peer5 for landmark Turing-completeness result).

Rafler's continuous-domain generalization of Conway's Life (floating-point cells, disk-averaged neighborhoods) producing smooth self-propelling glider-like blobs; the direct precursor to Lenia, less adopted and no evolution/learning. Source verified (arXiv:1111.1567, Stephan Rafler, 'Generalization of Conway's Game of Life to a continuous domain - SmoothLife'). Scores defensible vs Lenia anchor (morph3/vis4 sit just below Lenia's 4/5).

Kellie Evans' large-neighborhood birth/death generalization of Life whose organic 'bugs' and gliders converge to a proven continuum limit (RealLife); rich morphology and a key Lenia ancestor but no heredity, learning, or ecology. Verified real/distinct (Evans' large-radius life-like CA, a documented Lenia precursor). Scores mirror SmoothLife and are defensible; source is Evans' continuum-limit paper.

Plantec/Hamon/Etcheverry/Oudeyer/Moulin-Frier/Chan mass-conserving Lenia extension with localized in-CA parameters, enabling multi-species interaction and explicitly targeting open-ended evolution; strong novelty/ecology/morphology, weak explicit heredity+selection. Source verified (arXiv:2212.07906, 'Flow-Lenia: Towards open-ended evolution in cellular automata through mass conservation and parameter localization'). open4 defensible (OEE-targeting via intrinsic parameter localization, below interactive-novelty Picbreeder/ASAL 5). NOTE: could not confirm the 'ALIFE 2023 Best Paper' claim in the basis text — trimmed that unverifiable assertion; core system and scores stand.

Mordvintsev et al. (Google) reformulation of Lenia as particles minimizing an energy field, forming diverse self-organizing cell-like structures; highly polished visuals and embodied-ish dynamics but no reproduction, mutation, or learning. Verified real/distinct (Alexander Mordvintsev's Particle Lenia, the referenced Observable 'from scratch' notebook is a canonical live implementation). repro1/cog0 correct; scores defensible.

Schmickl, Stefanec & Crailsheim self-propelled particles under one motion law that self-organize into cell-like structures with membranes/nuclei, growth, decay, and binary-fission self-replication (Nature Scientific Reports 2016, srep37969); real emergent reproduction+population dynamics, no learning or open-ended genetic novelty. Source verified via PubMed 27901107 / Sci Reports. repro3 defensible (genuine emergent fission but no heritable genome/mutation, correctly below Langton's Loops 4); peer4 justified by Nature venue + 2D/3D followups.

Pearson's (Science 1993) two-species U+2V->3V reaction-diffusion system whose spots grow and divide by fission, producing iconic spots/stripes/maze Turing patterns; visually rich morphogenesis but a chemical-pattern PDE, not agents/heredity/evolution. Source verified (Robert Munafo's Gray-Scott exposition page, which documents Pearson's 1993 classification and spot-division dynamics). repro1 correct (fission without heredity), substrate1 correct (models chemistry but is a single PDE substrate, NOT multi-substrate); scores hold.

VERIFIED real (Aevol/Aevol-9, INRIA/Beslon group; Artificial Life & ECAL papers, bioRxiv 2025). Forward-time simulator of genome architecture: population with explicit circular bacterial-like genome, transcription/translation to protein-triangles fitted to a target curve; strong heredity + rich genome-architecture instrumentation, no bodies/physics/cognition. Fixed-target fitness => open_endedness=2. Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real (MSU; Avida compiled to JS via Emscripten, browser GUI; 2017 ISAL Education & Outreach Award confirmed). Educational front-end to Avida: self-replicating Avidians mutate and compete for resources, with live visualization, data export and lessons. Inherits Avida's heredity(5)/ecology(4), simplified for teaching. Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real. Adam Ierymenko's minimal single-file C ALife VM; ChrisHixon/nanopond is a legitimate fork 'based on Adam Ierymenko's original' (Ierymenko-lineage basis accurate). Tiny self-replicating programs on a 2D memory pond compete for space/CPU, mutate and evolve; strong replication, minimal tooling, no embodiment/cognition. Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real (SourceForge project physis; also physis.sourceforge.net). Maintainer username confirmed as 'sirna' (NOT Tim Taylor) via project page. A-Life research VM to study evolvability of assembly-like languages, explicitly aiming to be more general than Tierra/Avida; self-replicators evolve via mutation + fitness culling. Thinly documented => confidence 0.55 apt. Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real. Tim Taylor's virtual-OS simulating thousands of parallel self-reproducing programs modelled loosely on cellular organisms, aimed at multicellularity/Cambrian-explosion questions (PhD thesis Edinburgh 1999; 'Studying Evolution...' paper 1997 matches era). Tierra-lineage heredity, early OEE focus, no bodies/physics. Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real. First & canonical system classified open-ended by Bedau-Packard evolutionary-activity stats, and the ONLY closed system besides Earth's biosphere to pass the enhanced ALife Test (Channon). 'Maximum Individual Complexity is Indefinitely Scalable in Geb' (Artificial Life 25(2), 2019) confirms open_endedness=5. SAGA-based agents with developing neural nets (cog=4) under biotic selection. morph=1 (minimal 2D world, no real physics). Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real. Pargellis, 'Digital Life Behavior in the Amoeba World', Artificial Life 7(1):63-75, 2001 (source_url resolves to the exact article). Tierra-class world where a soup of random machine instructions (32-op universal set) self-organizes and spontaneously emits self-replicators competing for memory/CPU; notable for de-novo emergence of replication, no embodiment/cognition. Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real (Ken Stauffer, in development since 1996; rubberduck203/Evolve is the maintained open-source fork the source_url points to). 2D universe where uni-/multi-cellular organisms grow, move, eat, reproduce, controlled by an evolvable Forth-like language (KFORTH) as DNA with unbounded program size. Embodied 2D behavior (morph=2) + program-level control (cog=3, genetic-programming not neural — upper-bound but within tolerance). Scores rubric-sound.

type: 2D artificial chemistry with evolvable self-replicators (Tim Hutton)
basis: VERIFIED real. Hutton, 'Evolvable Self-Replicating Molecules in an Artificial Chemistry', Artificial Life 8(4):341, 2002; timhutton/squirm3 repo confirmed. Typed/stateful atoms move in a 2D grid and form bonds, giving DNA-like template-copying self-replicators that emerge from a soup and mutate. CORRECTED substrate_pluralism 2->1: this is a SINGLE artificial-chemistry substrate (typed atoms on a CA grid), not multiple distinct computational substrates per the rubric (unlike OpenWorm's genuinely-multiple modeling substrates=3). The 2D physics/chemistry earns morph=3. Other scores sound.

type: distributed Tierra with evolving multicellularity (Thomas Ray)
basis: VERIFIED real (Tom Ray, 1995; Network Tierra FAQ + evolving-digital-ecological-network literature). Internet-distributed Tierra: metapopulations of self-replicating assembly organisms migrate across networked soups with gene flow, seeded with a two-cell-type multicellular ancestor to probe the single->multicellular transition (succeeded in a designed 2-cell-type survivor, did not evolve added cell types). Strong heredity(5)/ecology(4), no embodiment. Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real (davidrmiller/biosim4, 2021; README confirms genome->neural-net brain, 2D arena, per-generation survival criterion). Each creature's genome compiles to a neural net wiring sensor- to action-neurons; selection sculpts behavior each generation. Strong genome->NN cognition(4), fixed-target (not open-ended, =2) selection, popular but non-peer-reviewed (peer=2). Scores rubric-sound.

VERIFIED real (Lipson & Pollack, Nature 406:974-978, 2000, 'Automatic design and manufacture of robotic lifeforms'; DEMO Lab site live). Evolutionary computation in rigid-body physics sim designs ~20-block bar-and-motor locomoting machines, then auto-3D-prints them — first robotically-designed-and-fabricated robots. Single locomotion target (open2), no inter-agent ecology (eco0), simple neural control (cog2), single digital substrate (substrate1), no consciousness mechanism (consc0). Scores defensible vs Karl Sims anchor; peer5 for foundational Nature paper.

VERIFIED real (Hiller & Lipson, Creative Machines Lab; 'Dynamic Simulation of Soft Multimaterial 3D-Printed Objects', Soft Robotics 1(1):88-101, 2014; jonhiller/VoxCAD + Voxelyze on GitHub, LGPL). Open-source voxel soft-material physics engine (nonlinear relaxation, per-voxel density/stiffness/Poisson/friction) — the de-facto substrate under evosoro/Cheney evolutionary-soft-robot work, not an evolver itself, hence low repro/open/cognition. Single substrate (substrate1), no consciousness (consc0). morph5+instr4 defensible.

VERIFIED real (Lee Graham; Wikipedia + Alchetron + dbpedia). GA/GP evolution of jointed rectangular-block 3D body plans with oscillator+sensor controllers under physics; fitness for distance/height/transport, changeable mid-run fitness for env change + lineage duplication for speciation (open3). CORRECTED ecology 2->1: sources describe SOLO fitness optimization, no genuine multi-agent interaction/coevolution/resources (below Avida eco4, unlike Karl Sims box-grabbing co-evolution). NOTE: 'distributed-volunteer' framing in original basis is unconfirmed — sources describe a single-machine hobbyist program; confidence lowered to 0.78. Single substrate (substrate1), no consciousness (consc0).

VERIFIED real (Gupta, Savarese, Ganguli, Fei-Fei Li, 'Embodied Intelligence via Learning and Evolution', Nature Comms 2021; arXiv 2102.02202; code agrimgupta92/derl). Tournament-based evolution of UNIMAL morphologies with per-agent deep-RL (PPO) in MuJoCo across hills/steps/rubble; demonstrates a morphological Baldwin effect. CORRECTED ecology 2->1: morphologies are independently evaluated on terrain tasks — no coevolution, shared resources, or food web (well below Avida eco4). cog5+morph5 correct; single substrate (substrate1); no consciousness mechanism (consc0).

VERIFIED real (the UNIversal aniMAL kinematic-tree design space introduced with DERL, arXiv 2102.02202, reused by MetaMorph to train one transformer over hundreds of morphologies). NEAR-DUPLICATE of the DERL row (same source paper) but distinguished as the design-space/benchmark component vs the DERL evolutionary framework — confidence lowered to 0.72 to reflect the overlap. CORRECTED ecology 2->1 (component/benchmark, no multi-agent interaction). Single substrate (substrate1); no consciousness (consc0). As a component its standalone reproduction (repro3) and peer standing (peer3) are lower than DERL.

VERIFIED real (Cheney, MacCurdy, Clune, Lipson, 'Unshackling Evolution: Evolving Soft Robots with Multiple Materials and a Powerful Generative Encoding', GECCO 2013; cited DOI 10.1145/2661735.2661737 is the 2014 SIGEVOlution reprint of the same GECCO'13 paper — resolves correctly). CPPN-NEAT generative encoding evolves multi-material (soft/actuated/rigid) voxel bodies in Voxelyze, scaling to 20^3; landmark in soft-robot evolution. Strong embodiment+heredity; control baked as volumetric actuation (cog1). eco1 (solo), single substrate (substrate1), no consciousness (consc0). Scores defensible.

VERIFIED real (Zhao, Xu, Konakovic-Lukovic, Hughes, Spielberg, Rus, Matusik; MIT CSAIL/CDFG; ACM TOG 39(6), SIGGRAPH Asia 2020; code allanzhao/RoboGrammar; cdfg.mit.edu page live). A graph grammar generates ~hundreds-of-thousands of articulated morphologies explored by Graph Heuristic Search with MPC control, optimized per terrain. Generative search (not Darwinian mutation/selection) so repro2. CORRECTED ecology 2->1: single-robot terrain design search, no multi-agent interaction. Single substrate (substrate1); no consciousness (consc0). morph5+instr4 defensible.

VERIFIED real (Yuan, Song, Luo, Sun, Kitani, 'Transform2Act: Learning a Transform-and-Control Policy for Efficient Agent Design', ICLR 2022 Oral; arXiv 2110.03659; code Khrylx/Transform2Act; NVIDIA research page). A single graph-based RL policy first applies transform actions to grow morphology then controls it, jointly optimizing body+behavior in MuJoCo (discovers giraffe/squid/spider-like designs). Learned-policy design rather than population EA (repro3), cognition high (cog5), headless visuals (vis2). eco1 (solo), single substrate (substrate1), no consciousness (consc0).

VERIFIED real (Wang, Liao, Ba, Fidler, 'NerveNet: Learning Structured Policy with Graph Neural Networks', ICLR 2018; U Toronto/Uber; OpenReview id S1sqHMZCb). GNN parameterizes control by message-passing over a robot's body graph (e.g. CentipedeEight), enabling zero-shot size/disability transfer. A control-learning method over FIXED morphologies with NO heredity/mutation/selection — correctly scored repro0 (marginal A-Life inclusion as embodied agent control, scored honestly). eco1, single substrate (substrate1), no consciousness (consc0).

VERIFIED real (Wang et al., 'Neural Graph Evolution: Towards Efficient Automatic Robot Design', ICLR 2019; OpenReview BkgWHnR5tm; arXiv 1906.05370). Evolutionary search over graph-structured morphologies with GNN-parameterized policies and Graph-Mutation-with-Uncertainty; discovers fish (fins+tail) and cheetah bodies. Genuine mutation/selection (repro4) plus learned control (cog4), headless MuJoCo. eco1 (no multi-agent interaction/coevolution), single substrate (substrate1), no consciousness (consc0).

VERIFIED real (Peter Krcah, 'Evolving Virtual Creatures Revisited', GECCO 2007; complete reimplementation of Karl Sims with diversity-encouraging EA + grafting/crossover, ODE physics, distributed fitness evaluation, evolved ~15cm creatures jumping ~7cm; cited in 'The Surprising Creativity of Digital Evolution'). Standard Newtonian-physics Sims-style creatures. The 'electromagnetic creatures' framing in the original basis was NOT confirmable in any source — no EM-substrate credit given (substrate1), confidence held at 0.55. eco1, no consciousness (consc0).

Genetic algorithm (Stanley & Miikkulainen 2002) that evolves ANN weights AND topology via innovation-number crossover, speciation, and incremental complexification — strong heredity/mutation/selection, learning-agent focus, but headless and single-substrate. Source verified: canonical, widely cited.

Hypercube-based NEAT (Stanley, D'Ambrosio & Gauci 2009) evolving large-scale ANNs via CPPN indirect encoding that exploits task geometry/regularities; applied to evolved-controller and 2D-substrate tasks, fundamentally an EA over ANN controllers. Real, distinct system.

Evolvable-substrate HyperNEAT (Risi & Stanley 2010-12) that derives hidden-node placement/density from the infinite-resolution CPPN weight pattern via quadtree decomposition; a more brain-like ANN-evolution extension, computationally heavy, narrower adoption than HyperNEAT. Real, distinct extension.

Lehman & Stanley (2008 ALIFE / 2011 ECJ 'Abandoning Objectives') — reward behavioral novelty alone, ignoring the objective; foundational open-endedness/divergence method (maze + biped physics experiments) that seeds the QD lineage; EA-based, headless. PDF source verified (maze/biped sections present).

Mouret & Clune (2015) illumination algorithm keeping the best elite per cell of a behavior-descriptor grid; the sister Nature 2015 hexapod damage-recovery work gives real embodiment, and its archive is a gold standard for QD instrumentation/reproducibility. Source verified.

Pugh, Soros & Stanley (2016) 'Quality Diversity: A New Frontier' formalizing the class (NS-with-local-competition, MAP-Elites) that returns a maximally diverse archive of high performers; strong benchmarks/instrumentation, applied to neuroevolution and robot morphology, but a method family rather than a single embodied world.

Ecoffet, Huizinga, Lehman, Stanley & Clune (arXiv 2019; Nature 2021 'First return, then explore') — archives promising states, returns then explores; solved Montezuma's Revenge/Pitfall. RL not an EA (weak heredity → repro 1), but a cell-archive descendant of QD/novelty thinking with strong learning agents and Atari-scale visuals. Source verified.

project: OMNI (Open-endedness via Models of human Notions of Interestingness)
basis: Zhang, Lehman, Stanley & Clune (2023) uses a foundation model as a 'model of interestingness' to prioritize tasks that are both learnable AND interesting, directly targeting the open-endedness quantification problem; a curriculum/task-generator over RL agents, not an EA, still early adoption. Title/authors verified via WebFetch.

Brant & Stanley (GECCO 2017, 'Minimal Criterion Coevolution: A New Approach To Open-Ended Search') coevolves mazes and maze-solvers where each need only satisfy a minimal reproduction criterion — no fitness/behavior descriptor, yielding indefinitely growing maze complexity; genuine two-population coevolution. Title/authors verified via WebFetch.

Nguyen, Yosinski & Clune (GECCO 2015 best paper, 'Innovation Engines: Automated Creativity and Improved Stochastic Optimization via Deep Learning') couples MAP-Elites-style QD with a deep neural network as the interestingness/behavior judge to auto-generate an ever-expanding set of recognizable images; strong divergent generation and rich visual artifacts, single substrate, no ecology. Paper/DOI/best-paper verified via search.

Clune & Lipson (2011, EndlessForms.com) 3D sibling of Picbreeder — CPPN-encoded objects bred by human selection (NEAT), collaboratively shared/rated by users and 3D-printable; strong open-ended novelty and 3D visual/embodied form, but selection is human (no in-agent cognition → cog 1) and it is a web artifact-breeder, not a benchmarked platform. PDF present at source URL (host yosinski.com verified).

World's most-used ABM platform (Wilensky 1999); many interacting turtle/patch agents on a 2D grid, BehaviorSpace parameter sweeps and reproducibility tooling, ships classic ALife models (Wolf-Sheep, ants) but heredity/evolution is optional per-model, not built-in.

GMU discrete-event multi-agent toolkit scaling to ~1M agents with strict model/visualization separation, checkpointing and reproducible parallel runs; used for swarm robotics and machine-learning experiments (some cognition), but no built-in heredity or physical body simulation.

Recursive Porous Agent Simulation Toolkit (U. Chicago/Argonne); mature ABM framework with built-in genetic-algorithm and regression adaptive features (some cognition), strong scheduling/logging/batch instrumentation and HPC (Repast HPC) scale, but reproduction is a library option not the model, and agents are non-embodied.

MIT Media Lab Logo-derived language for decentralized systems (Resnick/Klopfer); many turtle agents on patches produce emergent flocks/ants/traffic, education-focused so lighter measurement tooling and no built-in evolution or embodied physics; StarLogo Nova adds 3D.

Brookings-origin Java framework of composable 'scapes' (a scape is itself an agent) with built-in statistical monitoring; the canonical host for Sugarscape-style social models — strong for society/ecology on a lattice, but non-embodied, no built-in heredity, narrower adoption than NetLogo/MASON.

UMMISCO GIS-native ABM platform (GAML language) running up to millions of spatially-embedded agents with 2D/3D multi-layer visualization, BDI cognitive-agent architectures and rich inspection/experiment tooling; strong society + geospatial scale, but no built-in evolution and physics limited to spatial movement, not rigid/soft bodies.

Open-source Python ABM framework (the Python NetLogo/MASON alternative) with grid/network spaces, DataCollector + BatchRunner for reproducible sweeps and browser-based visualization; ships Schelling/Wolf-Sheep and integrates with the pandas analysis stack, but no built-in heredity or embodied physics.

C++ library by Craig Reynolds (of Boids) providing steering behaviors (seek/flee/flock/path-follow) for embodied 'vehicle' agents with a real-time OpenGL demo; genuine ALife lineage and simple embodied kinematics, but no heredity/evolution, minimal measurement tooling, and reactive not learning agents.

Axtell/Epstein/Gumerman household-level model of Kayenta Anasazi in Long House Valley 800-1350 AD; households reproduce/split and choose farm plots on a paleoenvironmentally-reconstructed landscape, empirically validated against the archaeological record (strong instrumentation/docking) — but a fixed single model, no genetics or open-endedness.

Thomas Schelling's 1971 grid model where agents relocate when too few neighbors match, showing macro-segregation emerges from mild individual preference; a foundational, heavily-cited emergence demonstration but a single fixed rule-set — no heredity, learning, physics, or open-ended novelty.

W. Brian Arthur's 1994 bounded-rationality model where agents hold competing predictors and adaptively switch strategies to attend an over-crowdable bar (generalized as the Minority Game); real adaptive/inductive cognition and rich collective dynamics, but disembodied, no heredity, and essentially non-visual.

Maxis god-game: player-guided 'evolution' across 5 stages with procedurally-generated, animated, physically-rigged creatures; but design is authored, not heritable/selected, so it's evolution-themed rather than real A-Life. VERIFIED: real, famous title; scores defensible (repro=1 correct since morphology is authored not inherited; consc=0, substrate=1 correct).

Maxis A-Life sim (Karakotsios): players tune genetics + environmental parameters and watch plants/herbivores/carnivores mutate, reproduce and evolve in a food web; abstract top-down grid, no embodied physics. VERIFIED real; genuine genetics+food-web justifies repro=3/eco=4; consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Will Wright Gaia-hypothesis planet sim: atmosphere/biosphere/life-class dynamics over geologic time to evolve sentient life; systems ecology with coarse biome/life classes and no per-organism heredity or bodies. VERIFIED real; CORRECTED ecology 4->3 (coarse biome classes with no per-agent food-web interaction — the basis itself notes 'coarse biome classes'; 3 is the defensible systems-ecology level, below SimLife/SimAnt agent ecologies at 4). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Will Wright agent-based ant-colony sim with pheromone-trail stigmergy, foraging, caste roles and inter-colony/predator competition; educational biology model but fixed rules, no genetic evolution. VERIFIED real; stigmergy/multi-colony competition justify eco=4, fixed-rules justify repro=1/open=1. consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

FOSS (GPL, Godot/C#) Spore-successor: a species progresses from a physically-simulated microbe via an editor + auto-evo population model with a compound/chemistry system; ambitious 7-stage scope but only microbe/multicellular stages are playable. VERIFIED FOSS + auto-evo + physical microbe stage confirmed on official wiki; scores defensible. consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Saterskog (Petter Saterskog) mobile sim of programmable few-celled organisms with a ~20-cell-mode genome, physically-simulated soft-body cells, and real mutation/selection in editable petri-dish environments; genetic but no neural cognition. VERIFIED on official site (dev + 16 cell types / 20 cell-modes-per-genome + true mutation-driven evolution confirmed); repro=4/morph=4 defensible, cog=0 correct (no NN). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

3D emergent natural-selection sim (dev Quasar): DNA-blueprinted creatures with sensory/behavioral traits speciate under real mutation+selection in a coevolving ecosystem, with clade-diagram / satellite-map / web-of-life tracking tools; solo-dev Early Access (stalled since ~2021). VERIFIED on Steam; real natural-selection engine + phylogeny tools justify repro=4/eco=4/instr=3. consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Caussan 2D digital-life sim where creatures with an evolvable neural-network brain + gene-based genome reproduce and evolve predation/parasitism/signalling under natural selection; players can inspect each Bibite's NN brain and genome. VERIFIED via official site + Steam (custom evolutionary + neural-network algorithms, predation/parasitism confirmed); cog=4 justified by evolvable NN brains (peer with OpenWorm=4). consc=0 correct (NN evolution is not a wired consciousness theory); substrate=1 correct.

Open-source 2D particle-physics sandbox (air pressure, heat, electricity, gravity, reactions); an A-Life-adjacent cellular substrate people build machines/CA in, but it has NO heredity, reproduction or selection, so it is not an evolution system. VERIFIED real + distinct; boundary case honestly scored (repro=0/eco=0). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Roguelite on the 'Falling Everything' engine where every pixel/material is simulated (liquids, gases, fire, thermodynamics); a rich per-pixel cellular-automaton physics world but no evolution, heredity or agent reproduction. VERIFIED real + famous; boundary case (repro=0), morph=4/vis=5 justified by per-pixel physics + polish. consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Emergent Garden (Max Robinson) grid cellular-automaton where multi-cell organisms (mouth/producer/mover/killer/eye/armor cells) eat, reproduce and mutate under true natural selection with NO fitness function; abstract 2D grid, no physics or neural cognition. VERIFIED on GitHub (author + cell types + explicit 'no manually-selected fitness, true natural selection' confirmed; GPL-3.0); repro=4 justified, cog=0/morph=1 correct. consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

VERIFIED (ventrella.com/Clusters loads; Jeffrey Ventrella's asymmetric particle system with per-type attraction/repulsion producing emergent cells/swarms; seeded the 'particle life' genre incl. Tom Mohr's Particle Life). No heredity/learning/bodies. peer_maturity 3->2: influential but a non-peer-reviewed indie art/code project, not academically adopted (rubric reserves >=3 for peer-reviewed/adopted). consciousness_theory correctly 0; substrate 1.

Max Bittker's browser falling-sand CA (~20 elements) in Rust+WASM/WebGL with a shareable public gallery; element-interaction emergence, no heredity/agents/learning. Scores consistent with a material-physics CA (morph=3 cell-level gravity/fluid, no bodies). consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct. Real, distinct system.

VERIFIED (video id r_It_X7v-1E = 'Coding Adventure: Simulating an Ecosystem', published 2019-06-10; foxes/rabbits in Unity, rabbits have heritable traits that evolve, predator-prey r/K dynamics). 3D kinematic bodies, food-driven sensing, no learned brains. eco=4 matches Avida anchor; open=2 (bounded trait drift) generous but within tolerance. consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct.

Pezzza's from-scratch C++/SFML ant colony: agents deposit/follow pheromone markers so collective foraging trails emerge via stigmergy; embodied 2D swarm, no heredity in core sim (separate NN/evolution experiment exists). cog=1 (gradient-following, not agent-internal learning) borderline vs 0 but defensible. source_url is the creator's channel (individual ant-sim videos live there); confidence 0.72 reflects that. consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct.

VERIFIED (video id 0ZGbIKd0XrM = Primer/Justin Helps 'Simulating Natural Selection', 2018; Blender/Python blobs with heritable speed/size/sense, mutation, food-limited selection, plus altruism/green-beard scenarios). Educational, simple sense-driven behavior, no learned controllers. eco=3 slightly generous (single-species food competition, not a food web) but within tolerance. consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct.

VERIFIED (github.com/keiwando/evolution; Karl-Sims-inspired Unity sandbox, jointed bone/muscle creatures with evolved NN brains via GA against physics tasks run/jump/climb/fly; save/share, cross-platform). morph=5 (real physics, embodied) and cog=3 match Karl Sims anchor. CORRECTED instrumentation 4->2: it is a consumer sandbox with fitness plots/save-share, NOT benchmark/receipt/reproducibility tooling (rubric 4=Avida-level, 5=Evolution Gym) — original 4 overstated by ~2. consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct.

VERIFIED (docs.openworm.org/Projects/DevoWorm; OpenWorm/Orthogonal Research Lab offshoot modeling C. elegans embryogenesis via developmental dynamics, digital morphogenesis on Morphozoic/CompuCell3D, evo-devo; open-science notebooks/repos). morph=4 slightly high for a mostly data-driven/headless project (OpenWorm=5 has a full biophysical body) but within tolerance. substrate=2 (data-bioinformatics + CA/reaction-diffusion modeling) borderline but defensible, not >2. instr=4 (reproducibility) justified. consciousness 0 correct.

VERIFIED (Portegys et al., 'Morphozoic, Cellular Automata with Nested Neighborhoods as a Metamorphic Representation of Morphogenesis', IGI Global 2017, pp.44-80; nested-neighborhood morphogenetic fields; demonstrated apps: Conway Life, cell regeneration, gastrulation sequence, neuron pathfinding, Turing reaction-diffusion; robust/noise-tolerant). Semantic Scholar page is JS-blank but the paper is confirmed real via IGI Global/ResearchGate. Scores consistent with a developmental CA. consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct.

VERIFIED (UCF ETD 5965 = Lisa Soros PhD 2018, advisor Kenneth Stanley; 'first scientific endeavor to systematically test an OEE framework in an alife world' — enumerates hypothesized necessary conditions for open-ended evolution, introduces Chromaria (CPPN-encoded coloring creatures self-place/interact), and shows stagnation when a condition is removed). open=4 defensible as a foundational OEE testbed (below ASAL/POET=5). consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct.

VERIFIED (Spector, Klein & Feinstein, Hampshire College, GECCO-2007; 3D physically-simulated blocks that grow/shrink, divide, form joints, exert force and trade resources under co-evolved RNN controllers; energy-conserving photosynthesis world; reliable emergence of cooperative resource transactions). Scores defensible vs Karl Sims/Avida anchors (repro=4 GA+dev, morph=4 3D physics, cog=3 RNN). consciousness 0, substrate 1 correct.

Verified: Wang et al., 'Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models.' GPT-4-driven lifelong Minecraft agent with automatic curriculum, ever-growing executable skill library, and iterative self-verification prompting; explores open-endedly but no heredity/self-replication and Minecraft is a blocky world, not a rigid-body physics sim. consc=0 correct (no GWT/IIT/HOT); substrate=1 correct (single digital substrate).

Verified: Park et al., 'Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior' (UIST 2023). 25 LLM agents in a sprite town with memory-stream, reflection, and planning produce emergent social coordination (Valentine's party, etc.); strongest ecology in the bucket but no reproduction, no physics, and no consciousness-theory mechanism wired in (consc=0). Open-endedness bounded (fixed social sim). substrate=1 correct.

Verified: Faldor, Zhang, Cully, Clune, 'OMNI-EPIC: Open-endedness via Models of human Notions of Interestingness with Environments Programmed in Code.' Foundation models write code for endless novel+learnable environments and reward functions (model-of-interestingness), training embodied agents in a physics sim; field-leading open-endedness (5, matches Clune open-endedness lineage) with real physics, but RL policies (no heredity, repro=1) and single agent (eco=1). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

type: Foundation world model / learned interactive environments
basis: Verified: Bruce et al. (DeepMind), 'Genie: Generative Interactive Environments' (ICML 2024). 11B model trained unsupervised on internet video that generates action-controllable playable worlds via a learned latent-action space and autoregressive dynamics; high visual fidelity (4) and learned world-physics (3), but a generator (no evolution/ecology/embodied bodies; repro=0, eco=1). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Verified: Lehman, Gordon, Jain, Ndousse, Yeh, Stanley, 'Evolution through Large Models.' Uses a code LLM as an intelligent mutation operator inside MAP-Elites to evolve hundreds of thousands of Python programs producing ambulating Sodarace robots (2D physics), then bootstraps a conditional model; genuine heredity/selection (repro=4) and embodied artifacts, single agent (eco=1). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

CarperAI open-source library operationalizing ELM: LLM mutation operators for quality-diversity search over code and natural language across example environments (incl. Sodarace); real QD heredity (repro=3) and strong reproducibility tooling (instr=4), but mostly headless (vis=1) and single-substrate. consc=0, substrate=1 correct. Real, distinct implementation library (not a duplicate of ELM the paper).

type: Meta-agent evolutionary search over agent code
basis: Verified: Hu, Lu, Clune, 'Automated Design of Agentic Systems.' Meta Agent Search programs new LLM agents against an ever-growing archive of prior discoveries, inventing designs that beat hand-crafted agents and transfer across domains/models; archive-based heredity (repro=3) and open invention, but disembodied (morph=0) and no live ecology (eco=1). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Sakana pipeline that ideates, codes, runs ML experiments, writes full papers, and auto-reviews them, iterating open-endedly like a research community (~$15/paper); strong open-ended discovery loop (4) and receipts (instr=4), but the system itself does not reproduce (repro=1) and is disembodied (morph=0). consc=0, substrate=1 correct. Real, distinct high-profile 2024 system.

Verified: Zhang, Hu, Lu, Lange, Clune, 'Darwin Godel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents.' Self-improving agents rewrite their own code, maintaining an expanding Darwinian archive of diverse variants selected by empirical benchmark gains (SWE-bench 20->50%, Polyglot 14->31%); field-leading open-ended evolution (open=5, title-confirmed) with real heredity (repro=4), but disembodied (morph=0) and non-social (eco=1). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

DeepMind (Fernando et al.) method that evolves a population of task-prompts while self-referentially evolving the mutation-prompts that mutate them, beating CoT/Plan-and-Solve on reasoning benchmarks; real mutation/selection heredity (repro=3) but fitness targets fixed benchmarks (open=2, correctly bounded, not open-ended). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.

Verified: Bradley et al., 'Quality-Diversity through AI Feedback.' Evolutionary QD loop where an LLM both generates variation and evaluates quality+diversity via AI feedback, producing diverse creative writing (stories/poetry/opinions) validated against human judgment; genuine QD heredity over a diversity grid (repro=3), disembodied (morph=0) and non-social (eco=0). consc=0, substrate=1 correct.
